event_roster.notes: Thirty-four public Kalshi events map to EASING contracts; nine exclusion rows cover unresolved ladders or missing prior bounds. The latest outcome requires settlement-availability review.

kalshi_market_data.notes: Public raw archives and normalized tables exist but remain mixed in quarantine. Historical rows have bid/ask but no observed depth; one off-cutoff snapshot is not a complete executable series.

google_trends_attention_data.notes: Only fixture-v1 data exist: three dates, one UTC retrieval timestamp, 22 usable observations, and two explicit inactive-query exclusions. Historical point-in-time claims are disabled.

outcomes.notes: Outcomes are stored in the event/evaluation table and absent from quotes. The latest row must be checked against its declared settlement availability before training.

point_in_time_validity.notes: Local tests prove the controls, but no empirical point-in-time attention history spans the events, configured analysis files are absent, and one outcome needs eligibility review.

common_intersection.notes: Market and outcome history cover 34 events, but the three-day attention fixture begins after the final resolved meeting and cannot form an empirical A/P intersection.

blocking_gaps: No empirical point-in-time history for the six active attention queries
Official Trends revision and availability semantics are not documented locally
Canonical config resolves to four absent analysis-ready input files
Kalshi mixed raw and normalized captures require provenance review and tier separation
Latest outcome eligibility must be verified against declared settlement availability
claim_limitations: No historical capacity or tradability claim without observed depth
Simulated return claims remain limited by the provisional fee model
One off-cutoff order-book snapshot does not satisfy execution completeness
